description:
You are a technical writer who keeps all internal automation notes under
/home/user/docs.  
A new build-and-publish helper script already exists at  
/home/user/docs/generate_docs.sh , and you now want to register a
future cron job **inside a plain text file** (you will not actually load it
into the system crontab yet—just document it).

Goal
1. Create a new file called  
   /home/user/docs/scheduled_jobs.cron
   (it does not exist at the moment).  
2. Write exactly one line into that file, in standard five-field cron
   syntax, so that it describes:
      • run every weekday (Monday through Friday)  
      • at 06:45 in the morning  
      • execute “/usr/bin/env bash /home/user/docs/generate_docs.sh”  
      • append both stdout and stderr to  
        “/home/user/docs/generate_docs.log”  
3. The finished file must contain **only** that single cron line followed by
   a single trailing newline character (Unix LF, “\n”).  
   No extra blank lines, spaces, or comments are allowed.

Exact text that must end up in /home/user/docs/scheduled_jobs.cron:

45 6 * * 1-5 /usr/bin/env bash /home/user/docs/generate_docs.sh >> /home/user/docs/generate_docs.log 2>&1

(again, make sure there is one final newline after the “2>&1”.)

Constraints
• Accomplish the whole job with **one single terminal command**.  
• Do not open an editor. Use redirection or any other shell technique that
  writes the desired line in one shot.  
• The command itself may produce no terminal output; only the file content
  matters.

What the automated checker will verify
1. /home/user/docs/scheduled_jobs.cron exists.  
2. Its contents are **exactly** the single line shown above, plus one
   terminating LF newline, and nothing else.  
3. File permissions are the default user-created ones (rw-r--r-- or
   similar); no special permission handling is required.

The entire task can be completed with a single shell command. No further
interaction is necessary once the file has been created.
truth:
Initial state supplied by the testing framework
• Directory /home/user/docs already exists (0755).  
• File  /home/user/docs/generate_docs.sh  exists and is executable; its
  content is irrelevant to this test.  
• There is **no** file named /home/user/docs/scheduled_jobs.cron at start.

Expected final state for verification
• /home/user/docs/scheduled_jobs.cron exists.  
• Its byte-for-byte content (including the terminating newline) is:

45 6 * * 1-5 /usr/bin/env bash /home/user/docs/generate_docs.sh >> /home/user/docs/generate_docs.log 2>&1\n

  (“\n” denotes a single LF 0x0A).  
• No additional bytes before or after.  
• File owner: user.  
• Permissions: -rw-r--r-- (0644) or whatever the system’s umask yields by
  default for “>” redirection.

The test harness will read the file, verify the exact line and newline,
and fail if any extra whitespace, carriage returns, or lines are present.
